title: Missing Key
description:
The function `word_frequency(words)` should take a list of words and return a dictionary mapping each word to how many times it appears.

It **crashes on the very first word** every single time. The error happens on a line that looks perfectly logical.

Why does Python refuse to increment something that doesn't exist yet?
hints: Try `word_frequency(["hello"])`. What error do you get? Which line causes it? Think about what `freq[word]` does when `word` is not yet a key in `freq`.
`freq[word] += 1` expands to `freq[word] = freq[word] + 1`. Reading `freq[word]` raises a `KeyError` if the key doesn't exist — which it doesn't the first time any new word is encountered.
Use `dict.get(key, default)` to safely read with a fallback: `freq[word] = freq.get(word, 0) + 1`. If the word isn't in the dict yet, `.get` returns `0` instead of raising an error.
